## What is Onyxia SSPCloud?

- [**Browser-based data science workspace**]{.orange} — no local installation
- Launch [**Python, R**]{.blue2} environments with [**CPU / GPU**]{.blue2} allocated on demand
- Built on [**Kubernetes**]{.orange} → scalable, reproducible, cloud-native
- [**Integrated S3 object storage**]{.blue2} — or connect to an external S3 bucket
- [**Project spaces**]{.orange}: share services & data across a team

::: {.notes}
**Onyxia SSPCloud** is a data science workspace accessible directly via your web browser. It allows you to launch development sessions (Python, R, etc.) using **on-demand CPU and GPU resources**, without requiring any local installation. The platform is built on **Kubernetes**, ensuring flexibility and scalability.

It features **integrated S3 object storage** for storing and accessing data. You can also connect to an external S3 bucket to retrieve third-party data.

You can request the creation of an **Onyxia "project"** to share services and data among multiple users. In our case, we have a project dedicated to the Satellite Imagery initiative, as well as an isolated **sandbox**—this is the environment where I will conduct my demonstrations to avoid interfering with production services and data.
:::

## Services Used in Our Project

<!-- *[Show Onyxia service catalogue]* -->

- [**VSCode / VSCode GPU**]{.orange}
  - Browser-based IDE, central tool for development & experimentation
  - GPU variant for deep learning training (SegFormer fine-tuning)

- [**MLFlow**]{.orange}
  - Experiment tracking: metrics, hyperparameters, artefacts
  - [**Model Registry**]{.blue2}: promote a run to `Production` with one click

::: {.notes}
Here are the Onyxia services we use in our use case:

*[Show Onyxia service catalog]*

- **VSCode / VSCode GPU**: An integrated development environment accessible via the browser. The GPU version enables deep learning model training by leveraging graphics accelerators. It is our core tool for development, debugging, and experimentation.

- **MLFlow**: A machine learning model lifecycle management platform. It allows us to track experiments (metrics, hyperparameters, artifacts), compare runs, and deploy models to production via its **Model Registry**.
:::

## Services Used in Our Project - 2

- [**Argo Workflows**]{.orange}
  - Kubernetes-native pipeline orchestration
  - DAG-based: each step is a container, dependencies are explicit
  - Enables [**parallel processing**]{.blue2} across image tiles

- [**ArgoCD**]{.orange}
  - GitOps continuous deployment
  - Watches our Git repo, auto-syncs the cluster on push
  - Used to deploy & scale our [**inference API**]{.blue2}

::: {.notes}
- **ArgoCD**: A GitOps-based continuous deployment tool. It monitors a Git repository and automatically synchronizes the Kubernetes cluster state with the declared configuration. We use it to deploy and update our inference API.

- **Argo Workflows**: A Kubernetes workflow orchestration engine. It allows us to run complex computational pipelines (training, inference) in a parallelized and reproducible manner, directly on the cluster.
:::
